content: Bash safety pattern: set -euo pipefail at top of script. -e exits on any command failure. -u treats unset variables as error. -o pipefail makes pipeline exit code equal the rightmost non-zero exit in the pipeline. Sourcing .env: the set -a; source .env; set +a pattern exports all variables from .env while skipping comments. This is safe for .env files with KEY=VALUE format including quoted values. Alternative: grep -v '^#' .env | grep -v '^$' | while IFS='=' read k v; do export "="; done — handles more edge cases. For non-interactive cron safety: scripts must not read from stdin, must not prompt for passwords, and must exit with non-zero codes on all failure paths. Use trap ERR for cleanup.
